name: Winston Lin
title: AI, software, and business systems
tagline: I'm Winston. I work across serverless, platform products, and business systems, and I also advise and invest in smaller businesses and startups. This site brings together case studies, writing, and Python tools from that work.
meta_description: Selected case studies, writing, and Python tools across serverless, platform products, business systems, and operator work.
intro_paragraphs: I’ve led product across serverless, CI/CD, and developer platforms, and I also work with operators and founders on growth, service design, and business systems.
experience_highlights: Principal Product Manager for OCI Functions, focused on developer experience and execution model
Previously led product work across OCI DevOps / CI/CD and adjacent platform systems
Built internal platform capabilities that shortened delivery cycles and reduced operational drag

intro: I’m based in San Francisco and work in product, mostly around cloud and developer tools. Day to day, I spend a lot of time thinking about how to make things simpler and easier to use, especially for people building and shipping products.

Outside of that, I invest in and occasionally advise early-stage startups in both the U.S. and Asia. I like being around people building things from scratch and thinking through how something goes from an idea to something that actually works.
facts: Work in product across cloud and developer tools
Invest in and advise early-stage startups
Grew up between Taiwan and Orange County
personal: I grew up between Taiwan and Orange County, which gave me a mix of perspectives early on and made me comfortable moving between different environments. I later studied at UC Berkeley Haas, where I got more interested in the overlap between business and technology.

Outside of work, I try to stay active and get outdoors when I can, whether that means hiking around the Bay Area, exploring new places, or just getting out of the city for a bit. I also shoot photography on Fujifilm, mostly as a way to slow down and capture things that feel real in the moment.
I grew up playing sports and still play basketball and volleyball when I can. Lately I’ve also been spending more time thinking about investing, real estate, and how small decisions add up over time.
